description: Machine-readable catalog of data-collection capabilities exposed by the Solo Agency Local Collector. AI agents in playbooks read this list to decide which capability fits a user's request, then submit a collector job whose source specifies the chosen capability id plus inputs. The collector runs the matching extractor and returns typed records. Facebook screens use the internal-GraphQL provider (richer, structured); every other site falls back to generic HTML extraction. Collection is read-only and runs only under the user's explicit command on accounts and pages the logged-in user can already access.

usage.how_agents_choose: Match the user's intent against each capability's 'when_to_use' and 'output_schema'. Prefer a Facebook GraphQL capability whose status is 'stable' or 'beta'; if none fits, submit the source without a capability id and the collector applies generic HTML extraction.
usage.how_to_invoke: POST /jobs/run_now with sources[] where each source is { "url": "<screen url>", "capability": "<capability id>", "inputs": { ... } }. Read results from the run's private_data_points.jsonl; typed output is in the record's 'records' field, keyed by the capability's output_schema.
usage.orchestration_note: Multi-step work (visiting many profiles, friend-of-friend traversal, filtering by industry, writing reports) is the playbook agent's job. Each capability only reads ONE screen into typed data.
usage.status_meaning: stable=live-verified; beta=unit-verified, live pending; planned=not built; not_graphql=this screen is not served by GraphQL interception (server-rendered / interaction-gated) and needs a DOM/HTML approach or an alternative capability.
usage.pagination: Deep collection via cursor replay: set inputs.max_pages (default 8, max 40) on any paginated capability (group.posts, group.search_posts, groups.search, people.search, profile.posts, profile.friends, newsfeed). Instead of relying on scroll to load more, the collector replays the captured query with each end_cursor to pull further pages directly. Example: fb.group.search_posts with max_pages:10 returned 66 posts vs 6 without.

EntityRef.type: string — one of profile | group | page | event
EntityRef.name: string — display name

PostRecord.post_id: string — numeric post id when present
PostRecord.actor: EntityRef — the posting actor (named 'actor', NOT 'author': the bridge redacts any field whose name contains 'auth')
PostRecord.engagement: object { reactions, comments, shares } | null — counts when visible, null when not surfaced
PostRecord.group: EntityRef | null — the containing group/page when applicable

ProfileSummary.name: string
ProfileSummary.subtitle: string — tagline/work line shown on the row when present
ProfileSummary.industry_hint: string | null — coarse hint if surfaced on the row

AboutRecord.contact: object — public contact fields the profile shares

ReelRecord.creator: { name, url } | null — the reel author
ReelRecord.caption: string — visible caption text (includes creator name + hashtags)
ReelRecord.hashtags: array of #tags parsed from the caption

WebResult.title: string
WebResult.url: string — real destination url (empty for ads)

ProfileHeader.name: string
ProfileHeader.category: string — e.g. Realtor / Real Estate Agent
ProfileHeader.website: string — external site link when shown
ProfileHeader.cta: array of button labels (Book Now, Send Message, ...)

ReactionResult.status: string — done | already | dry_run | not_found | error
ReactionResult.reaction: string — like | love | care | haha | wow | sad | angry
ReactionResult.verified: boolean — reaction confirmed present after the click
ReactionResult.target_preview: {author, snippet, url} — so the report can confirm the right post

CommentResult.text: string — the comment that was posted
CommentResult.verified: boolean — composer cleared after submit (post confirmed)
CommentResult.appeared: boolean — the text was found in the on-page comment list

capabilities.title: Facebook group posts (feed)
capabilities.when_to_use: Read recent posts from a Facebook group the logged-in user can access — for lead scanning, idea mining, or monitoring discussion inside a group.

inputs.max_scroll: int (optional) — how many feed pages to load
inputs.max_pages: int (optional, default 8, max 40) — deep pagination via cursor replay

capabilities.coverage: logged-in user's accessible view of the group

capabilities.title: Search posts inside a Facebook group (by keyword)
capabilities.when_to_use: Find all posts inside ONE group that match a keyword — search a community group for posts expressing a need/intent/product/service, to mine leads. Returns the matching posts.

inputs.max_pages: int (optional, default 8, max 40) — deep pagination via cursor replay

capabilities.coverage: posts in the group matching the keyword, visible to the logged-in user

_impl.edge_path: data.serpResponse.results.edges → rendering_strategy.view_model.click_model.story (identical story-node shape as group_feed; reuses postRecordFromStoryNode)
_impl.note: Use the /search/?q= form, NOT /search/posts/. Entity results (groups/people) excluded by requiring click_model.story.
_impl.validated_on: 2026-07-15 (live verified on aven-ngo — keywords nha, cho thue, all matched)

capabilities.title: Facebook profile friend list
capabilities.when_to_use: Read the friend list of a given profile (subject to that profile's privacy). Use as the first step of friend / friend-of-friend audits and network mapping.

inputs.profile_url: string (required) — the profile whose friends to read
inputs.max_pages: int (optional, default 8, max 40) — deep pagination via cursor replay

capabilities.coverage: only visible when the target profile shares its friend list

_impl.graphql_query: ProfileCometAppCollectionNonSelfFriendsListRendererPaginationQuery (also the Self variant); scoped to captures whose query name contains 'Friends'

node_fields.name: edge.node.title.text
node_fields.subtitle: edge.node.subtitle_text.text
node_fields.mutual_friends: parsed from subtitle when it is a mutual-friends line

capabilities.title: Facebook profile About / bio
capabilities.when_to_use: Read a single profile's About section — work, education, current city, hometown, public contact, bio text — to classify a person (e.g. infer industry).
capabilities.coverage: only fields the profile makes visible to the logged-in user

_impl.note: About is a single-node response (not an edges connection); several sections load lazily and need their own queries.
_impl.finding: 2026-07-15: structured About fields (work/education/city) are NOT available via GraphQL interception — server-rendered. The captured ProfileCometAppSectionFeedPaginationQuery carries only nav sections + pageItems urls, no field text. Needs a dedicated About-tab DOM parser.
_impl.recommended_alternative: For inferring a person industry (e.g. FoF lead filtering), call fb.people.search with the person name — the SERP row returns their occupation subtitle + industry_hint. Also the friend vanity url often encodes the trade (e.g. edsocalrealtor).

capabilities.title: Facebook group search results
capabilities.when_to_use: Find Facebook groups matching a keyword — for discovering communities to join and scan.

inputs.max_pages: int (optional, default 8, max 40) — deep pagination via cursor replay

_impl.graphql_query: SearchCometResultsPaginatedResultsQuery (shared SERP; scope by name containing SearchComet)

capabilities.title: Facebook people search results
capabilities.when_to_use: Find people matching keywords (persona-based lead discovery). Returns row-level summaries, not full profiles.

inputs.max_pages: int (optional, default 8, max 40) — deep pagination via cursor replay

capabilities.title: Facebook profile posts (timeline)
capabilities.when_to_use: Read a profile's or page's own posts (timeline feed) — for monitoring a person, competitor, or page.

inputs.max_pages: int (optional, default 8, max 40) — deep pagination via cursor replay

capabilities.coverage: logged-in user's accessible view of the timeline

capabilities.title: Facebook home newsfeed
capabilities.when_to_use: Read the logged-in user's home feed. Note the feed mixes many story types (ads, suggestions, reels, memories); expect noisier output.

inputs.max_pages: int (optional, default 8, max 40) — deep pagination via cursor replay

_impl.edge_path: data.viewer.news_feed.edges[].node (skip ads/suggestions: require comet_sections + story message)

capabilities.title: Facebook post comments
capabilities.when_to_use: Read the comments on a specific post — for mining leads or sentiment from a discussion thread.

inputs.post_url: string (required) — a permalink to the post

capabilities.output_schema: { post: PostRecord, comments: [{ author: EntityRef, text, created_time }] }
capabilities.coverage: comments visible to the logged-in user

_impl.finding: 2026-07-15: no comment GraphQL query fires on a passive permalink scroll. The first page of comments is server-rendered (initial document/RSC payload); only load-more PAGINATION uses GraphQL (CometUFICommentsProviderQuery), which needs enough comments to trigger. Needs HTML/RSC parsing of first-page comments, or a comment-pagination trigger.

capabilities.title: Facebook Reels feed (creator + caption + hashtags)
capabilities.when_to_use: Collect people from the Reels feed by industry: the agent reads each reel caption/hashtags to detect a trade (real estate, insurance, mortgage, ...) and collects the creator. NOTE: DOM-driven (creator/caption are NOT in GraphQL) — it advances the Reels player and scrapes each displayed reel.

inputs.reel_url: string (required) — a https://www.facebook.com/reel/<id> to enter the feed
inputs.max_reels: int (optional, default 20, max 80) — how many reels to advance through and scrape
inputs.debug: bool (optional) — include a DOM sample for tuning

capabilities.coverage: reels shown in the logged-in user's personalized reels feed while advancing

_impl.method: DOM: advance player via ArrowDown + scroll, scrape reel-link cards for creator link + caption text + hashtags
_impl.note: GraphQL FBUnifiedVideoContainerQuery paginates reel URLs but carries NO caption/creator (deferred render module, confirmed across 16 chunks), so this is DOM-based.
_impl.validated_on: 2026-07-17 absellsaz/reels/ → 60 reels with views (2.8K…190K) via grid mode
_impl.grid_mode: profile reels TAB (/…/reels/) → scrape a[href*="/reel/<id>"] cards: reel_id + view count overlay (parseCount) + url; infinite-scroll, stop after 2 dry scrolls. Player mode (/reel/<id>) still advances + scrapes caption/creator.
_impl.graphql_for_captions: ProfileCometAppCollectionReelsRendererPaginationQuery (captured; future extractor for captions like fb.profile.videos)

capabilities.title: Web search (DuckDuckGo / Google / Bing) — result links + snippets
capabilities.when_to_use: Search the OPEN WEB (beyond Facebook) for a query — a person, email, business, or topic — and get organic result links + titles + snippets. Use to find someone's public web presence (personal site, social profiles, listings, directories) for enrichment, or general research. Ads are filtered out by default.

inputs.url: string (required) — the search engine results URL. DuckDuckGo (default, safest): https://html.duckduckgo.com/html/?q=<enc>. Google (richer, but higher CAPTCHA risk at volume — use for targeted enrichment): https://www.google.com/search?q=<enc>. Bing: https://www.bing.com/search?q=<enc>. The extractor auto-detects the engine by host.
inputs.include_ads: bool (optional, default false) — keep sponsored results too

capabilities.coverage: public web search results (DuckDuckGo HTML endpoint)

_impl.method: DOM, engine auto-detected by host: DuckDuckGo (.result rows, uddg-decoded url), Google (external a[href] wrapping an h3 — robust to class churn), Bing (li.b_algo). Ads filtered.
_impl.note: DuckDuckGo HTML chosen for clean, parseable markup + low bot-detection vs Google. First result page only (no pagination yet). To read a RESULT PAGE's content, submit its url with NO capability — the generic HTML engine returns readable text (raw_visible_text_excerpt).
_impl.validated_on: 2026-07-16 (live DDG 10 results; Google DOM parses 9 organic in in-app browser)
_impl.engines_note: DuckDuckGo default = clean markup + low bot-detection (best for bulk). Google = best coverage but can trigger 'unusual traffic' CAPTCHA when hit at volume; prefer for low-volume, high-value lookups. Runs in the user's real logged-in Chrome, which lowers (but does not remove) that risk.

capabilities.title: Facebook profile/page header (followers, verified, website, CTA)
capabilities.when_to_use: Read a profile/PAGE header — follower count, verified badge, category, external website, CTA buttons, and whether it has Reels/Videos tabs. This is the TOP opener for outreach personalization ('your page has N followers, a verified badge…'). Submit the profile's main URL.
capabilities.coverage: the logged-in user's view of the public profile/page header

_impl.method: DOM/SSR (no clean GraphQL header query, like About): text patterns for 'N followers'/'N likes', verified marker, category words; first external non-FB link = website; /reels + /videos link presence; CTA from role=button labels.
_impl.note: Server-rendered; robust to class churn by keying off text/links. One record = the profile.
_impl.validated_on: 2026-07-16 discovery on absellsaz (followers 10K, verified, Realtor, hasReels confirmed in DOM)

capabilities.title: Facebook page Videos tab (title, views, reactions)
capabilities.when_to_use: Read a page's Videos tab — per video: title, VIEW count, reactions, caption, url. Use for 'your video X with 77K views' outreach hooks and to find a page's best-performing content. Submit the page's /videos URL.

inputs.max_pages: int (optional, default 8, max 40) — deep pagination via cursor replay

capabilities.coverage: the logged-in user's view of the page's videos

_impl.validated_on: 2026-07-16 discovery on absellsaz/videos (raw text shows 2.3K–12.7K views per video)

capabilities.title: React to a Facebook post/video/reel (like, love, …)
capabilities.when_to_use: WRITE ACTION. Add a reaction (default love/like) to a single post, video, or reel. Submit the post PERMALINK as the url. Approval is handled upstream by the daily report — only create this job for content the operator has approved. Idempotent: if the post is already reacted, it is left unchanged.

inputs.reaction: string (optional, default like) — like | love | care | haha | wow | sad | angry
inputs.dry_run: bool (optional) — locate + preview the target WITHOUT clicking (debug only; not the approval gate)

capabilities.coverage: the logged-in operator reacts as themselves via the real UI

_impl.method: DOM automation (MAIN world, gql_actions.js): locate the main post UFI react toggle by locale-aware aria-label (EN+VI); for a non-like reaction, hover to open the flyout and click the option; real pointer+mouse event chain; verify via aria-pressed / Remove-label after click.
_impl.idempotent: if already reacted, no-op (clicking Like again would REMOVE it); P1 does not switch an existing reaction to a different one.
_impl.note: One action per job. Pacing/caps/approval live in the flow layer + daily report, not in this capability.
_impl.audit: result appended to outbox/actions_log.jsonl by the bridge
_impl.validated_on: 2026-07-17 real like on reel/4524074751166406 → done/verified; drift-guard + scrollSteps=0 for writes

capabilities.title: Comment on a Facebook post / video / reel
capabilities.when_to_use: PAUSED (2026-07-17) — being redone as a dedicated multi-platform (Facebook/X/Reddit) commenting module focused on DOM editors + text-input mechanics. The current DOM impl works on reels/permalinks (execCommand insertText + Enter), but match_caption profile-targeting is flaky (use direct permalinks). Do not rely on it in the daily-report flow yet.

inputs.post_url: string (required) — permalink: /watch/?v=, /<user>/posts/<id>, /permalink.php?..., /reel/<id>; OR a profile URL when using match_caption
inputs.match_caption: string (optional) — when post_url is a profile/timeline, scroll to the post whose caption contains this text and comment on THAT post
inputs.dry_run: bool (optional) — locate composer + preview WITHOUT posting (debug only; not the approval gate)

capabilities.coverage: the logged-in operator comments as themselves via the real UI

_impl.method: DOM automation (gql_actions.js): resolve scope (whole page, or the article matching match_caption); find the comment composer (contenteditable[role=textbox], EN+VI aria-labels), open it if collapsed; type via execCommand insertText (fallback beforeinput/input); submit with Enter; verify the composer cleared.
_impl.note: One comment per job. Pacing/caps/approval live in the flow layer + daily report.
_impl.audit: result appended to outbox/actions_log.jsonl by the bridge
